title: Restore image playlist truth on preset and hosted loads
summary: 0.6.21 keeps the 0.6.20 image-directory pause and focus-loss snapshot work, but removes stale hosted playlist reuse and lets new presets carry their real playlist so image next/previous behavior stays truthful again.
changes: Store the current player playlist in new presets instead of saving only the selected media path.
Restore preset media using the saved playlist order when it is available, rather than collapsing directory-backed image state to one file.
Stop hosted loads from reviving an older multi-item playlist when the incoming request is narrower than the current record.
Stamp version 0.6.21 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and deploy the 0.6.21 raw modern_tv dev_deploy proof seam and baseline direct-player raw asset.
reasoning: The remaining basics regression was playlist truth, not the next/previous stepper math itself.
A preset that was saved from a real playlist should restore that same playlist order instead of silently degrading to one selected file.
A narrower hosted load request should replace stale playlist state rather than inheriting a broader earlier playlist.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Image next and previous should stay consistent when the current state came from a directory-backed image load or a new preset saved from that state.
Hosted reloads should stop feeling like they are dragging old playlist order forward into a new narrower selection.
The 0.6.20 image pause and focus-loss snapshot improvements stay intact.
knownIssues: Older presets that were saved before playlist paths were recorded still cannot fully reconstruct a directory-backed playlist from file-only data.
Mixed-media playlist policy across images and videos is still a separate product decision beyond this basics fix.
